text.title: code-review-slo-and-rubric Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the code-review-slo-and-rubric methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team sets first_response_business_hours=4 with no data; PR latency stays at 36h; team loses trust in the SLO.
antipattern.root-cause: Author chose a target, not a baseline.
antipattern.fix: Source the SLO from current p50 + bump 20%; iterate quarterly.

antipattern.symptom: Reviewer blocks PRs on naming preferences; throughput drops.
antipattern.fix: Re-classify naming nits as non-blocking; require blocker citations against the rubric list.

antipattern.symptom: Team enables CodeRabbit and disables human review; security defect ships.
antipattern.fix: Restore mandatory human judgment pass; AI stays mechanical.

antipattern.symptom: One senior reviewer carries 18 open PRs; first-response p95 = 72h.
antipattern.fix: Add a CI bot or load-balancer that re-assigns when the reviewer is at the cap.
